title: last_seen_at = last_seen_at no-op in update_contact_fully SQL
description: UPDATE chat_contact SET blocked = ?, last_seen_at = last_seen_at WHERE id = ? contains a no-op self-assignment. SQLite does not auto-update timestamps, so last_seen_at = last_seen_at writes the same value back and has no effect. It misleads readers into thinking there is an implicit auto-update being suppressed (a pattern from MySQL's ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), and invites cargo-culting in future UPDATE statements. Remove it: UPDATE chat_contact SET blocked = ? WHERE id = ? is equivalent and cleaner.
close_reason: Fixed in code; decision records documented
